records.ety: [L. transfusus, p. p. of transfundere: cf. F. transfuser. See Transfund.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Transfused (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Transfusing.]

defs: To pour, as liquid, out of one vessel into another; to transfer by pouring.
To transfer, as blood, from the veins or arteries of one man or animal to those of another.
To cause to pass from to another; to cause to be instilled or imbibed; as, to transfuse a spirit of patriotism into a man; to transfuse a love of letters.
quotes: Into thee such virtue and grace Immense I have transfused.
